patch:
diff --git a/.github/workflows/build.yml b/.github/workflows/build.yml
index 941d635d2..e96c5a73e 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/build.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/build.yml
@@ -53,6 +53,8 @@ jobs:
             version: "41"
           - distro: fedora
             version: "42"
+          - distro: fedora
+            version: "43"
 
     steps:
       - name: Checkout
diff --git a/.github/workflows/ci.yml b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
index 77cc9c88c..c9414c109 100644
--- a/.github/workflows/ci.yml
+++ b/.github/workflows/ci.yml
@@ -374,7 +374,7 @@ jobs:
     strategy:
       matrix:
         distro: ["fedora"]
-        version: ["41", "42"]
+        version: ["41", "42", "43"]
     steps:
       - name: Checkout
         uses: actions/checkout@v6
@@ -487,6 +487,8 @@ jobs:
             version: "41"
           - distro: fedora
             version: "42"
+          - distro: fedora
+            version: "43"
 
     steps:
       - name: Checkout
diff --git a/CHANGELOG.md b/CHANGELOG.md
index a06e9e8c4..112a28944 100644
--- a/CHANGELOG.md
+++ b/CHANGELOG.md
@@ -53,6 +53,8 @@ since 0.4.1, and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.or
 
 - Add support for Ubuntu 25.10 (Questing Quokka)
   ([#1264](https://github.com/freedomofpress/dangerzone/issues/1264))
+- Add support for Fedora 43
+  ([#1263](https://github.com/freedomofpress/dangerzone/issues/1263))
 - Add support for the new Debian Testing (Forky)
   ([#1265](https://github.com/freedomofpress/dangerzone/issues/1265))
 - Drop support for Ubuntu Oracular (24.10) since it is end of life
diff --git a/INSTALL.md b/INSTALL.md
index a42a6039b..8710df386 100644
--- a/INSTALL.md
+++ b/INSTALL.md
@@ -77,6 +77,7 @@ Dangerzone is available for:
 - Debian 13 (trixie)
 - Debian 12 (bookworm)
 - Debian 11 (bullseye), support will be dropped in future releases.
+- Fedora 43
 - Fedora 42
 - Fedora 41
 - Tails
diff --git a/dangerzone/updater/releases.py b/dangerzone/updater/releases.py
index 439312973..bccb5a47c 100644
--- a/dangerzone/updater/releases.py
+++ b/dangerzone/updater/releases.py
@@ -53,6 +53,7 @@ def is_empty(self) -> bool:
         )
 
 
+@dataclass
 class EmptyReport:
     """Empty report, when there is nothing to report"""
 
diff --git a/dev_scripts/qa.py b/dev_scripts/qa.py
index c896332c7..4a0d9ee87 100755
--- a/dev_scripts/qa.py
+++ b/dev_scripts/qa.py
@@ -1005,6 +1005,10 @@ def build_package(self):
         )
 
 
+class QAFedora43(QAFedora):
+    VERSION = "43"
+
+
 class QAFedora42(QAFedora):
     VERSION = "42"
 
diff --git a/dodo.py b/dodo.py
index 2dbcee334..8c30fedd5 100644
--- a/dodo.py
+++ b/dodo.py
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@
 
 ARCH = "arm64" if platform.machine() == "arm64" else "i686"
 VERSION = open("share/version.txt").read().strip()
-FEDORA_VERSIONS = ["41", "42"]
+FEDORA_VERSIONS = ["41", "42", "43"]
 
 ### Global parameters

problem_statement:
Add support for Fedora 43
Fedora 43 will be released on early November, so we need to add support for it in 0.10.0
hints_text:
Supporting Fedora 43 requires adding support for Python 3.14, which is the default version in Fedora 43. Usually, this means that PySide6 is not supported, because it tends to lag behind the latest Python versions.

In this particular case, the Fedora project has [added support](https://packages.fedoraproject.org/pkgs/python-pyside6/python3-pyside6/) for Python 3.14 in `python3-pyside6`. Unfortunately, the same does not apply to the [PySide6 PyPI package](https://pypi.org/project/PySide6/), at least for now:
* https://wiki.qt.io/Qt_for_Python_Development_Notes
* https://bugreports.qt.io/browse/PYSIDE-2954?jql=text%20~%20%223.14%22

In theory, we should still be able to release a package for Fedora 43, by spawning a Fedora 43 container/VM, and running our build scripts manually there. The problem is that we won't have an easy way to test it locally and via our CI.

The [target release date](https://fedorapeople.org/groups/schedule/f-43/f-43-key-tasks.html) for Fedora 43 is in November 11, which is in about a month from now. I suggest we wait a bit until the PySide6 devs catch up in the meantime. Else, if the deadline approaches and the PySide6 don't make it in time, we'll have to build and test a Fedora package manually.
> I suggest we wait a bit until the PySide6 devs catch up in the meantime

We discussed that today and agreed on this. Let's wait until it's ready, and release `0.10.0` without Fedora 43 support for now
Note, the Fedora 43 release date has been confirmed to be October 28.
As Fedora 43 is out for more than 2 weeks now and Python 3.14 for over a month, but PySide still does not seem to have any plans to support the latest Python Version upstream, would it maybe be possible to release a Fedora 43 build nevertheless?
Hi, and thanks for asking @XSpielinbox. We plan to work on this next week, and 0.10.0 will be released for Fedora 43 in the coming weeks.
I'm happy to report that after playing a bit with `pyproject.toml`, I managed to create a Fedora 43 RPM that passes the manual QA. I haven't run the automated tests yet since I haven't tweaked the Poetry environment to use system packages. I'll try to do so soon.
PySide 6.10.1 was just released with support for Python 3.14: https://pypi.org/project/PySide6/
